source_pr.title: importinto: fix flaky test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #47416

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

ci might be slow sometimes, 10 second might not be enough, so set max wait to 30

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [x] No need to test
  > - [x] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->  

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Eventually timeouts too short for slow CI (10s) in importinto realtikv tests
failure_signature: importinto realtikv tests can exceed 10s Eventually timeout under slow CI
root_cause_explanation: This case is flaky because the importinto real-TiKV tests rely on asynchronous state transitions and use `require.Eventually(...)` with a timeout that was too aggressive for slow CI environments (10 seconds). When the cluster is under load, the expected condition may not become true within 10s, causing intermittent failures even though the logic is correct. The fix mitigates the flake by increasing the maximum wait time (e.g., 10s → 30s) while keeping the polling interval the same.
fix_pattern: Mitigate CI timing jitter by increasing `require.Eventually(...)` timeouts in importinto real-TiKV tests (e.g., from 10s to 30s) so async operations have enough time to complete under load.

evidence: evidence: increase `require.Eventually` timeout (10s→30s) for slow CI

analysis:
PR title: importinto: fix flaky test
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/47431
Triage sources: manual_triage, explicit_flaky_signal
Triage summary: increase `require.Eventually` timeout (10s→30s) for slow CI
Changed files (6): tests/realtikvtest/importintotest/detach_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/importintotest/import_into_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/importintotest/job_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/importintotest/one_parquet_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/importintotest/util_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/importintotest4/global_sort_test.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
